meaning: n.<主澳俚>(镶嵌贵重蛋白石用的)劣质蛋白石
examples: IT at many other Western Banks is often a hotch-potch of homemade systems.
In the opal cutting process the potch is ground away from the presentation areas of the gem opal.
A further 35% or so came from coal, less than 15% from nuclear power and the rest from a hotch-potch of other sources.
Mr Chirac's presidential manifesto in 1995 was a hotch-potch of contradictions and promises, many later broken.
Power deregulation is getting under way in earnest too, although states are embracing a hotch-potch of different approaches.
